title: zapateria hirsuta
side_effects: Zapateria hirsuta can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. If ingested in large quantities, it can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use or excessive exposure to the plant's oils can lead to skin irritation, including rashes, itching, and blistering. Additionally, improper use of the plant can also trigger allergic reactions, anaphylaxis, and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals.
